nameMeanings.name: Esther
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by a Jewish captive who became the wife of the Persian king Ahasuerus. Her Hebrew name was Hadassah ‘myrtle’, and the form Esther is said to be a Persian translation of this, although others derive it from Persian stara ‘star’. It may also be a Hebrew form of the name of the Persian goddess Ishtar. According to the book of the Bible that bears her name, Esther managed, by her perception and persuasion, to save large numbers of the Jews from the evil machinations of the royal counsellor Haman. See also Hester .

en.lifeSummary: Esther was born in 1550, in Poundisford Park, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with John Grisell. She died in 1604, in her hometown, at the age of 54, and was buried in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Estherは1550年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトポウンディスフォード パークで生まれた。彼女はJohn Grisellとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1604年に故郷で54歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Esther(은)는 1550년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 파우디스퍼드 박에서 태어났다. 그녀는 John Grisell(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1604년 그녀의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에 묻혔다.
